items.title: Hong Kong's 'sound of silence': 6 years under the national security law
items.description: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.

items.title: In pictures: Marine Le Pen’s political rise and frustrated presidential ambitions
items.description: French far-right figurehead Marine Le Pen could see her 40-year political career come to an end on Tuesday if French courts rule that she is guilty of embezzling public funds and ineligible to stand for the presidency in 2027. FRANCE 24 takes a look at how she rose to prominence in France and brought her National Rally party into mainstream politics.

items.title: Boston: The cradle of the American Revolution
items.description: Americans celebrated their independence on July 4th. But did you know that the revolution that led to the creation of the US began in Boston? We take you on a tour of this revolutionary city.

items.title: Huge crowd joins funeral procession for Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei
items.description: Enormous crowds gather in Tehran for third day to mourn amid calls for revenge against the US and Israel.

items.title: Avignon turns 80: Wagner Moura, Isabelle Huppert attend France's oldest theatre festival
items.description: FRANCE 24's Olivia Salazar-Winspear brings us all the latest from the Avignon performing arts festival, which is celebrating its 80th anniversary. This year, Golden Globe winners, Brazilian actor Wagner Moura and French actress Isabelle Huppert are starring in shows. The festival has also chosen Korean as its language of honour and turns the spotlight on artists from the Mediterranean region. Salazar-Winspear also talks to the co-director of Avignon's fringe program. It's known as the "off" in French and features more than 1,700 shows performed in public spaces around the city. Finally, she gives us her recommendation for a fringe show that is not to be missed.

items.title: Why FIFA’s Balogun red card suspension after Trump call is so controversial
items.description: FIFA's decision has led to a furore in the football world - and accusations of bending its rules to please Trump.

items.title: U.K.'s Farage referred to standards watchdog after new report of undeclared benefits
items.description: Mr. Farage’s spokesperson said ⁠the story was “baseless and contrived”

items.title: Solstice buys Element Solutions in $14.5 billion deal, sharpens focus on AI market
'Genuine admiration and emotion' in Tehran's streets as Iran mourns supreme leader
items.description: Vast crowds have gathered for the funeral procession of Iran's slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran on July 6, with authorities estimating millions were on the streets. The final public tribute is expected to last around 10 hours. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah tells us more on what to know.

items.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
items.description: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

items.title: Argentina seek to shake off 'Messi-dependence' against Mo Salah's Egypt
items.description: Lionel Scaloni's side escaped a major scare against Cape Verde, but their dependence on Lionel Messi remains a growing concern ahead of a last-16 meeting with Mohamed Salah's Egypt. Leer más

items.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet is in Tehran, where funeral events are taking place in honour of Iran's former leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Tanzania arrests dozens as security tightens ahead of planned protests
items.description: Tanzanian authorities have arrested dozens of people ahead of planned anti-government protests, intensifying a crackdown on dissent as activists demand democratic reforms and the release of jailed opposition leader Tundu Lissu amid heightened security across the country.

items.description: Germany been attracting many skilled workers to its labor market. The challenge, however, is that many of them do not stay. So what is going wrong?

items.title: Moldova speeds up its membership process to the EU
items.description: An economically fragile country caught up in Russia’s hybrid war, Moldova is keen to make rapid progress for a quicker membership to the European Union. With the opening of the first chapter of accession negotiations, the EU launched its €1.9 billion investment plan in the country. With this plan, which is conditional on domestic structural reforms, Europe hopes to draw in businesses and drive change in the country.

items.title: Uganda coffee exports hit by weaker prices
items.description: Uganda's coffee export earnings dropped sharply in May as falling global prices and lower export volumes weighed on the country's top agricultural export. The decline highlights the vulnerability of Africa's largest coffee exporter to swings in international commodity markets.

items.description: The UN General Assembly meets amid Israel's genocide on Gaza, Myanmar's genocide against the Rohingya, and other crises.

items.description: Across Iran, families are grappling with soaring living costs, as the combined weight of war, subsidy reforms and sanctions pushes basic necessities beyond the reach of many. A Syrian state news agency reported that France's Emmanuel Macron will visit to discuss strengthening bilateral relations. It wouldn't be the first time the two have met. In May 2025, Macron welcomed al-Sharaa to the Élysée Palace, where they discussed issues of security and Macron addressed the interreligious violence in Syria.

According to the country's public broadcaster, a 76-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of starting the wildfire, as he allegedly ignited the blaze after his vehicle produced sparks.

The centres provide shelter, daily care and psychological support while helping reduce the risk of infection.

items.title: UK scraps £45 million girls' education programme
items.description: Britain has scrapped a flagship £45 million programme aimed at helping one million girls across Africa, Asia and the Middle East gain access to higher education, just two years after it was announced.

items.title: Pope urges Europe to protect migrants in visit to island frontier
items.description: Pope Leo XIV has travelled to the Italian island of Lampedusa, one of Europe's main gateways for migrants crossing the Mediterranean. In a powerful appeal, the pontiff urged Europe to do more to protect and integrate those fleeing conflict and poverty.

items.title: ‘Very dangerous’ super typhoon nears U.S. Pacific islands
items.description: Super Typhoon Bavi was forecast to roar westwards over the area early on July 6 with maximum sustained winds of up to 280 kilometres (173 miles) per hour and gusts of 333 kmh, according to the Joint Typhoon Warning Centre

items.title: Microsoft joins AI-driven tech layoff wave with 4,800 job cuts
Milei-linked Fundación Faro told to name donors behind US$4.8mn windfall
items.description: Fundación Faro presented itself as a platform of ideas. But the libertarian think tank’s opaque finances, links to key Milei insiders and 400-fold surge in donations have left the organisation facing uncomfortable questions from regulators and judges. Leer más

items.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
items.description: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

items.title: Milei star guest at US Embassy’s 250th independence day celebration
items.description: Javier Milei first sitting Argentine president to attend US Embassy’s annual party in Buenos Aires; Ambassador Peter Lamelas hails relationship between two nations as United States marks 250 years of US independence. Leer más

items.title: ECB payment system suffers second outage in a week
England's Azteca thriller a stunning deliverance from ghosts of the past
items.description: England teams of old would have buckled under the pressure put upon them by Mexico and their 80,000-strong band of supporters at the Azteca. But this England team turned a nightmare into a dreamland, at a venue haunted by so many ghosts of the past.

items.title: Milei tries again to revive Argentina’s dormant mortgage market
items.description: Mortgage-backed home sales surged early in Milei’s Presidency only to evaporate as the government stemmed a currency run by raising interest rates above 100%. Leer más

items.title: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
items.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

items.title: Jury told accused used a 'Razor' scooter to kill axe-wielding man
items.description: A Supreme Court jury hears a Razor-branded scooter was the alleged murder weapon used to kill a man who was armed with an axe during a violent brawl north of Newcastle.

items.title: Santilli sworn in as Cabinet chief as Milei reshuffles pack
items.description: Diego Santilli formally took over as President Javier Milei's Cabinet chief on Tuesday, completing a transition triggered by Manuel Adorni's resignation amid a corruption investigation. Leer más

items.title: Morocco beats Canada to book a place in the World Cup quarter-finals
items.description: Following their 3-0 victory, the Atlas Lions will now face tournament favourites France, in Boston next Thursday.

items.title: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
items.description: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.

items.title: Lautaro Martínez or Julián Álvarez? Scaloni weighs Messi's strike partner for Cape Verde clash
items.description: Argentina's coach must decide who will lead the line alongside Lionel Messi as the world champions prepare for Friday's last-32 clash with Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: SA childcare centre closes for three months after 'very serious' allergy incident
items.description: South Australia's Education Standards Board says it has "serious concerns" about an Edge Early Learning centre after a child was taken to hospital following an allergen incident, with the centre directed to close immediately for the second time this year.

items.title: Algeria's ruling FLN secures most seats in parliament with historically low turnout
items.description: Algeria's ruling National Liberation Front won the most seats in the country's parliamentary elections, the election board said Monday, securing 90 out of 407 in the July 2 vote. But the poll was marred by record-low turnout after the government barred more than a third of prospective candidates from running.

items.title: Large crowds of mourners attend slain supreme leader's funeral in Tehran
items.description: Three of Ali Khamenei’s sons joined the mass prayers, but not his successor, Mojtaba, who has not been seen in public since he assumed the role.

items.title: Outrage as FIFA's Infantino bends rules to Trump's whim at World Cup
items.description: FIFA boss slammed for controversial decision to suspend US star Folarin Balogun's one-game ban; Ruling, which came after the intervention of Donald Trump, permits striker to play in the co-hosts' World Cup last 16 match with Belgium. Leer más

items.title: Dutse residents seek support after rainstorm damages homes
items.description: Some affected residents described the rainstorms as one of the most destructive in recent times The post Dutse residents seek support after rainstorm damages homes appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Mali creates state body to regulate gold trade
items.description: Mali has created a new state agency to regulate its booming artisanal gold sector after uncovering major discrepancies between officially declared exports and imports reported abroad.

items.title: Trump confirms call at centre of red card controversy 'with massive political consequences'
items.description: President Donald Trump confirmed he asked FIFA boss Gianni Infantino to review the "horrible" decision to hand a red card to star US striker Folarin Balogun but said he did not request it be overturned. Belgium, who the US will be playing in the quarterfinal, have called the decision 'incomprehensible' and a 'a blatant violation of the most basic rules of football and sport'. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes tells us more and whether this could change Belgium's chance of winning.

items.title: Argentina survive Cape Verde scare to reach World Cup last 16
items.description: Cristian Romero's header in injury time secures 3-2 victory over Cape Verde in extra time; African side impressed in hard-fought contest in Miami, but Argentina's resilience and never-say-die attitude saw them through. Leer más

items.title: Santilli's first test: Casa Rosada short of votes to scrap PASO primaries
items.description: Milei’s new Cabinet chief must negotiate with governors and opposition caucuses over controversial reform that would eliminate compulsory primaries. Leer más

items.title: More stars or stripes?
items.description: Within a decade of the Civil War, the US economy had overtaken Great Britain and the entire British Empire by around 1895 – from that point onward, it was more a question of the US impact on world history than the reverse. Leer más

items.title: Belgian coach says his comments were not a criticism of African football
items.description: Rudi Garcia said he was referring to teams unused to dealing with the kind of pressure that comes with managing a lead in high-level World Cup game

items.title: 'His resilience, his words, his character': Mourners grieve late supreme leader Khamenei
items.description: A sea of mourners filled the streets of Tehran on Monday for the funeral procession of late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. FRANCE 24 spoke to some of those who had travelled across the country to pay their respects for the man who led the Islamic Republic for almost four decades until he was killed in US-Israeli air strikes.

items.title: Iran's slain supreme leader lying in state in Tehran head of funeral events
items.description: Nearly four months after his death, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's burial is taking place as Iran and the US observe a fragile ceasefire.

items.title: Britain should consider regulating AI models, FCA official says
Arrogance as state policy and the end of Manuel Adorni
items.description: Manuel Adorni's resignation, in which he accused the press of "media attacks," comes across as pretty pathetic from a spokesman and Cabinet chief who built his tenure on arrogance and who now stands accused of corruption. Leer más

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: El-Obeid under siege by RSF: Could this be Sudan’s next el-Fasher?
items.description: UN warns of 'catastrophe' in el-Obeid after RSF atrocities in el-Fasher last year.

items.title: Iran begins farewell to Supreme Leader Khamenei
items.description: Before dawn, hundreds of mourners gathered in Tehran as Iran began days of funeral ceremonies for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed months earlier in a joint US-Israeli airstrike.

items.title: Iran: Millions flock to Ali Khamenei's funeral procession
items.description: Crowds have thronged the streets of Tehran in a procession for Iran's late supreme leader. The huge event is seen as a show of strength by the theocratic regime amid peace negotiations with the US.
